expressions.english:
to run somewhere
to run to a destination
expressions.notes:
빠르다 = to be fast
빨리 is an adverb form of 빠르다.

expressions.english: to run fast
expressions.notes:
높다 = to be high, tall
높이 is an adverb form of 높다.

expressions.english: to jump high
expressions.notes: 펄쩍 is an adverb that describes how someone jumps vigorously or outrageously denies something.

expressions.english: to jump (in a vigorous way)
to run slowly
expressions.notes: -(으)ㄹ까요? = Shall we + S + V?; Do you want me to + V?; Should we + V?; Let's + V.

expressions.english: We are late, shall we run?
expressions.notes: -(으)ㄹ 것 같다 = I think + S + will/be going to; it seems like + S + will/be going to

expressions.english: I think I will be late even if I run.
expressions.notes:
복도 = hallway, corridor
-(으)면 안 되다 = shouldn't; must not

expressions.english: You must not run in the hallway.
expressions.notes:
좀 = a little; please
더 = more
-(으)ㄹ 수 있다 = can, to be able to; there is a chance that + S + V

expressions.korean: 좀 더 빨리 뤼 수 있어요?
expressions.english: Can you run a little faster?
expressions.notes: 숨이 차다 = to be out of breath

expressions.english: I'm out of breath because I ran here.
I will quickly run over there and come back.
expressions.notes:
정말 = really; very
싫어하다 = to dislike, hate

expressions.english: I really don't like to run.
expressions.notes:
-마다 = every + N
-씩 is a particle used for the amount that is divided or repeated.

expressions.english: I run 10km every morning.

expressions.korean: 우리 이제 뛰어야 될 것 같아요.
expressions.english: I think we should run now.
expressions.notes:
급하다 = to be urgent, pressing; to be hasty
급하게 is an adverb form of 급하다.
-다가 = as a result of + V-ing
넘어지다 = to fall (down); to trip

expressions.english: I fell down because I was running in such a hurry to get here.
expressions.notes:
어디 = where; somewhere
그렇게 = like that; such + adjective/adverb

expressions.english: Where are you running off to in such a hurry?
expressions.notes:
힘들다 = to be difficult, hard, tough; to have a hard time; to be tiring; to be tired
그냥 = just, only, simply
걷다 = to go on foot; to walk to + place
-아/어/해요 = imperative ending; Let's + V

expressions.english: It's exhausting to run, so let's just walk.
expressions.notes:
-(으)ㄹ 필요 없다 = to not need to + V
-아/어/해도 되다 = to be okay to + V

expressions.korean: 뤼 필요 없어요, 그냥 걷어가도 돼요.
expressions.english: There is no need to run. We can just walk.
expressions.notes:
여기 = here, this place
거기 = there, that place
얼마나 = how + adjective/adverb
(시간이) 걸리다 = to take (a certain amount of time)

expressions.english: How long does it take to run from here to there?
expressions.notes:
약속 = plans; promise
시간 = time; hour
버스 = bus
내리다 = to get off (from a vehicle)
-자마자 = as soon as + S + V

expressions.korean: 약속 시간에 늦어서 버스에서 내리자마자 자 뛰어갔아요.
expressions.english: I had to run as soon as I got off the bus because I was late for my meeting.
